scope.summary: Add a Preserve Topology refit option (RefitMode::RedrapeOnly with topology_lock=true) that explicitly prohibits panel subdivision or edge count change during the re-drape pass, matching MD 2025.1 Preserve Topology option, and expose this flag in the refit_garment MCP tool
acceptance_criteria: RefitMode::RedrapeOnly gains an optional topology_lock: bool field (default false)
When topology_lock=true, the tessellation step is skipped; the solver must use the original panel mesh vertex count unchanged
The refit MCP tool's JSON schema reflects the topology_lock field via schemars::JsonSchema auto-generation
Validation adds a MESH_TOPOLOGY_PRESERVED check (Blocking): vertex count of output == vertex count of input when topology_lock=true
scope.risk_if_missed: Without topology lock, downstream tools that rely on stable vertex-count (skinning weight baking, morph target pipelines, blend shape applications) break when the refit changes mesh density. This is the most-requested refit option in MD 2025.1 and the primary adoption ask from game-engine pipelines.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
